            -- By definition, I ◁ x = x.
            exact by
              have h₆ : I ◁ x = x := by
                -- Use the property of the identity bird.
                exact IdentityBird.reflexivity x
              exact h₆
          exact h₅ x
        exact h₄
      exact h₃ x
    rw [h₂]
  have h₂ : x ◁ x = I ◁ x := by
    have h₃ : I ◁ x = x := by
      have h₄ : ∀ (x : Bird), I ◁ x = x := by
        intro x
        have h₅ : I ◁ x = x := by
          have h₆ : ∀ (x : Bird), I ◁ x = x := by
            intro x
            exact by
              have h₇ : I ◁ x = x := by
                exact IdentityBird.reflexivity x
              exact h₇
          exact h₆ x
        exact h₅
      exact h₄ x
    rw [h₃]
  have h₃ : x ◁ (I ◁ x) = I ◁ x := by
    calc
      x ◁ (I ◁ x) = x ◁ x := by rw [h₁]
      _ = I ◁ x := by rw [h₂]
  exact by
    simpa using h₃
  <;>
  try simp_all
  <;>
  try tauto

use M
      intro x
      -- By definition of the mockingbird, M ◁ x = x ◁ x
      -- We need to show that x ◁ (M ◁ x) = M ◁ x, i.e., x ◁ (x ◁ x) = x ◁ x
      -- This is equivalent to x ◁ x = x ◁ x, which is trivially true
      calc
        x ◁ (M ◁ x) = x ◁ (x ◁ x) := by
          rw [M.call]
          <;> rfl
        _ = x ◁ x := by
          -- Use the fact that x ◁ x = x ◁ x (identity)
          rfl
        _ = M ◁ x := by
          rw [M.call]
          <;> rfl
  <;> rfl

  end SummoningASagebird

have h₁ : I ◁ x = x := by
      -- We use the property of the identity bird that I ◁ x = x for any bird x.
      apply IdentityBird.I_after
    -- Substitute I ◁ x = x into the equation.
    simp [h₁]
    <;>
    aesop
  -- The proof is complete with the identity bird as θ.
  exact h
